report_name: Hong Kong Hospital Authority Investigates Major Data Breach Exposing Personal and Medical Data of Over 56,000 Patients

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Contractor
threat_actors.description: A third-party contractor suspected of inappropriately accessing and leaking sensitive patient data from the Hong Kong Hospital Authority's systems. The breach is being treated as an insider threat, as the actor likely used legitimate, privileged credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The contractor likely used their legitimate, privileged account, provided for system maintenance, to access the hospital's systems and patient data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The actor collected sensitive patient files and data, including PII and PHI, from the hospital's internal databases or information repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The attacker aggregated data for over 56,000 patients, suggesting an automated script or the use of powerful system reports was used for bulk data collection.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The compromised patient data was exfiltrated and discovered on an external third-party platform, indicating it was likely uploaded over the web.

identities.name: Hong Kong Hospital Authority
identities.description: A Hong Kong government agency responsible for managing public hospitals, which suffered a data breach affecting over 56,000 patients from its Kowloon East hospital cluster due to inappropriate access by a contractor.

identities.name: 56,000 Patients of Kowloon East hospital cluster
identities.description: A group of over 56,000 patients whose personal identifiable information (PII) and protected health information (PHI), including HKID numbers and surgical details, were exposed in the data breach.

infrastructure.name: Third-party data hosting platform
infrastructure.description: An external third-party platform where the sensitive patient data exfiltrated from the Hong Kong Hospital Authority was discovered.

indicators.name: Suspicious Bulk Patient Data Query
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Bulk Patient Data Query
id: 2b1c8e9f-a3d4-4f5e-8b6a-9c7d0e1f2a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects a database query that attempts to select all records from a table commonly containing patient data, which could indicate a bulk data exfiltration attempt as seen in the Hong Kong Hospital Authority breach.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEFMR5Tavhb_6Yot4fVTcT3ynkI_HsNW6D4O6nVRCwcBcKOtgv-erFkoolEctmh2JvJ-nGBD9MAzZAZjIKo_2X3qQMlCFFOgrZ_PnObyIDm6S0pnHW8aYAeJRiJW25CgY4MbBcMNvxatrVRjVOtzPxQ79Br0B2AHFNtbpdrhCnNuao1DCmrJNK2lMoApjSB83u-AQiCcjsUBIfaQ1hJumPWVkc0Ark-GOJ89CCUj9Sccttu7kHnHJUzIX0qlIo3HqsvrhNHSnwkyjdZ
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.t1213
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: database
detection:
  selection:
    query|contains:
      - 'SELECT * FROM patients'
      - 'SELECT * FROM patient_records'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative, reporting, or backup tasks.
indicators.description: Detects database queries attempting to select all records from tables likely to contain patient data, a behavior consistent with bulk data collection.

indicators.name: Anomalous Data Access by Vendor Account
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Data Access by Vendor Account
id: 8e9f0a1b-2c3d-4e5f-6a7b-8c9d0e1f2a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects when an account associated with a vendor accesses an anomalously high number of records, which could indicate abuse of privileged access for data exfiltration. This is a conceptual rule for UEBA or advanced audit log monitoring systems.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEFMR5Tavhb_6Yot4fVTcT3ynkI_HsNW6D4O6nVRCwcBcKOtgv-erFkoolEctmh2JvJ-nGBD9MAzZAZjIKo_2X3qQMlCFFOgrZ_PnObyIDm6S0pnHW8aYAeJRiJW25CgY4MbBcMNvxatrVRjVOtzPxQ79Br0B2AHFNtbpdrhCnNuao1DCmrJNK2lMoApjSB83u-AQiCcjsUBIfaQ1hJumPWVkc0Ark-GOJ89CCUj9Sccttu7kHnHJUzIX0qlIo3HqsvrhNHSnwkyjdZ
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: audit
detection:
  selection:
    user.group: 'vendors'
    action: 'read'
    object.type: 'patient_record'
  aggregation:
    c_records: count(distinct object.id) by user.name
    # This conceptual aggregation requires a UEBA system. The condition would be c_records > 1000 over a short time window.
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large-scale maintenance or data migration activities by vendors.
indicators.description: Identifies vendor accounts accessing an unusually high number of patient records, a key behavior in the described insider threat scenario that could be detected by UEBA systems.

relationships.target_name: 56,000 Patients of Kowloon East hospital cluster

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Data Access by Vendor Account
